channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Censurar redes sociais não vai salvar Lula
item.description: The article argues that censoring social media will not save Lula from his political challenges, claiming such a measure won't fix his popularity or legitimacy issues. It draws an analogy to Robert Mueller’s investigation into alleged Russian interference in the 2016 U.S. elections, which, despite widespread attention, failed to alter the electoral outcome or prevent Trump’s victory. The core message is that censorship is an ineffective and authoritarian response to crises of public perception.

item.title: Ronaldo Caiado é o entrevistado de hoje do programa 'Frente a Frente'
item.description: Ronaldo Caiado, former governor of Goiás and PSD presidential pre-candidate, was interviewed on UOL's 'Frente a Frente' program. He stated that his first act as president would be to grant a broad, general, and unrestricted amnesty to those convicted in the January 8 attacks, including Jair Bolsonaro. While his proposal mirrors that of Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, Caiado presents himself as a moderate alternative in the polarization between Lula and bolsonarism.

item.title: Humorista inspira ‘sem querer’ slogan para campanha de Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Comedian Murilo Couto unintentionally created a potential slogan for Flávio Bolsonaro’s campaign by using the phrase 'my friend Flávio' sarcastically in his shows and online videos. Despite the critical tone, Bolsonaro’s team adopted the phrase on X to gauge social media reception. Murilo, who is not a Bolsonaro supporter, took a trip to Africa after the joke went viral.

item.title: O que diz o Centrão sobre o avanço do PL na janela partidária
item.description: Centrão leaders attribute the PL's growth during the party switching window to the influence of Flávio Bolsonaro's surname, the party's pre-candidate for presidency, who is attracting politicians seeking competitiveness against Lula. Bolsonaro is seen as the strongest right-wing contender to challenge the current president in the 2026 election, even after the window closed without shifting the overall balance of power in Congress.

item.title: Lula ainda precisa definir articulador político, mas principal votação no Congresso depende dele
item.description: Lula has not yet defined his new political articulator in Congress after Gleisi Hoffmann's dismissal to run for Senate. The approval of Jorge Messias' STF nomination depends heavily on Lula's influence, especially in the Senate, where allies suggest choosing someone with strong congressional ties. Lula is also negotiating with House Speaker Hugo Motta on ending the 6x1 work scale and awaits talks with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre to advance Messias' nomination, whose referral to the CCJ will test the government-Senate relationship.

item.title: Nesta semana, duas agremiações veiculam propaganda partidária
item.description: This week, União Brasil and Republicanos are airing free political party propaganda on radio and television on January 7 and 11, 2026, between 7:30 PM and 10:30 PM. União Brasil will broadcast a total of one minute (30 seconds each day), while Republicanos will air three minutes (1 minute and 30 seconds each day), as established by TSE Ordinance No. 460/2025. Political party propaganda in the first semester of 2026 is restricted to Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Saturdays during prime time, with each party entitled to 20 minutes for the period.

item.title: Beneficiários de programas sociais sem cadastro biométrico ganham mais prazo para fazer nova identidade
item.description: The Ministry of Management and Innovation in Public Services has extended the deadline for issuing the new National Identity Card (CIN) for social program beneficiaries without biometric registration, pushing it to January 2027. Those with existing biometric data in the TSE, CNH, or passport have until January 2028 to obtain the CIN. The measure ensures no citizen is disadvantaged in accessing or renewing social benefits, allowing sufficient time for free biometric enrollment.

item.title: Guerra no Irã paralisa reaproximação de Lula com Trump e trava negociações comerciais
item.description: The U.S. and Israeli attack on Iran abruptly halted the diplomatic rapprochement between Lula and Trump, which had been slowly building since late 2025. The crisis froze bilateral trade negotiations and shifted Washington’s focus entirely to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Lei obriga empresas a informar trabalhadores sobre vacinação e garante folga para exames
item.description: President Lula signed a change to the CLT requiring employers to inform workers about official vaccination campaigns and diseases such as HPV and breast, cervical, and prostate cancer, also guaranteeing time off for preventive exams.

item.title: Eleições 2026: cidadão tem um mês para tirar título ou regularizar situação eleitoral
item.description: Brazilian citizens have until May 6 to obtain their voter ID, update registration data, transfer electoral domicile, or resolve pending electoral matters, after which the electoral roll will close for the 2026 elections. The deadline complies with Electoral Law No. 9,504/1997, requiring closure 150 days before the vote, set for October 4. After this date, voting or electoral regularization will no longer be possible.

item.title: JOTA Principal: Governo Lula estuda medidas econômicas a seis meses do 1º turno
item.description: The Lula government is studying economic measures six months before the first round of the 2026 elections, including a provisional measure to subsidize diesel and support the aviation sector, backed by most state governors. It also aims to curb fuel price increases amid Middle East tensions and Trump’s threats against Iran, while the PL gains strength in Congress and Kátia Abreu joins the PT.

item.title: STF analisa na quarta como devem ocorrer as eleições para mandato-tampão no Rio; entenda
item.description: The Brazilian Supreme Court (STF) will analyze on Wednesday (8) two actions regarding the election for governor of Rio de Janeiro, determining whether the interim governor until 2027 will be chosen by direct popular vote or indirect vote by state deputies. The state has been without a governor since Cláudio Castro’s resignation on March 23 and without a vice-governor since May 2025, with the succession line blocked by cassations and arrests. The Court will also assess the validity of legal provisions governing the indirect election, including candidacy incompatibility periods and vote secrecy.

item.title: Pedido de Flávio por união na direita após rixa entre Nikolas e Eduardo reforça tensão na família Bolsonaro na pré-campanha
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro called for unity on the right following a clash between Nikolas Ferreira and Eduardo Bolsonaro, highlighting family tensions in the 2026 pre-campaign. He criticized internal divisions while stating 'the enemy is over there', after Eduardo accused Nikolas of sharing profiles of those who do not declare support for his brother. Flávio’s effort to broaden alliances with figures like Sergio Moro, Deltan Dallagnol, and the Novo party has caused discomfort for Carlos and Eduardo, who fear it may dilute the core Bolsonarist identity.

item.title: A homenagem do Senado à primeira mulher brasileira a se alistar para votar
item.description: The Senate awarded the Bertha Luz medal to Gina Viana, granddaughter of Celina Guimarães Viana, the first Brazilian woman to register to vote, in recognition of her legacy as a suffragist in the fight for women's suffrage in Brazil. The honor was presented last week by Senator Zenaide Maia, underscoring the historical significance of Celina Guimarães Viana in advancing women's political rights in the country.

item.title: Janja prepara carne de paca para Páscoa de Lula; entenda prato
item.description: First Lady Janja prepared paca meat for Easter 2025, sharing a step-by-step cooking video on social media with President Lula present. Lula praised the dish as 'divine' and complimented Janja’s cooking. The post went viral on X, making 'carne de paca' a trending topic in Brazil.

item.title: Indígenas chegam a Brasília para o Acampamento Terra Livre
item.description: Thousands of Indigenous people arrived in Brasília for the Terra Livre Encampment, demanding demarcation of ancestral lands and opposing the 'time frame' thesis that limits their rights to territories occupied in 1988. The event, running until April 10, includes marches to Congress and the Presidential Palace, pressuring the Lula government to advance demarcation policies following the revocation of a decree expanding Amazon river ports for agribusiness.

item.title: Ação penal contra Eduardo Bolsonaro completa um ano parada no STF
item.description: A criminal case against Eduardo Bolsonaro for defamation against Tabata Amaral has been stalled for one year at the STF, ready for judgment after being released by Justice Nunes Marques. Court President Edson Fachin is responsible for scheduling the case, but there has been no progress since March 2025. The rapporteur is Alexandre de Moraes, and the PGR advocates for his conviction.

item.title: Lula mira o Sudeste no início do ano eleitoral
item.description: Lula focuses his early 2026 electoral year travels on the Southeast, with over 60% of his off-Brasília trips directed to São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, and Minas Gerais to solidify support in key states. He has secured declared backing for PT candidates in São Paulo (Haddad), Rio (Paes), and Espírito Santo (Helder Salomão), while Minas Gerais remains under negotiation with Rodrigo Pacheco (PSB). The Northeast also receives attention, with Salvador as a frequent destination, reinforcing the allied base in Bahia.

item.title: Agenda da semana: Artemis 2 chega perto da Lua
item.description: During the week of April 6–10, 2026, Brazil’s political agenda features the Artemis 2 mission’s lunar orbit flyby, the deadline for public sector salary adjustments (banned 180 days before elections), testimonies in the Organized Crime CPI involving former governors Claudio Castro and Ibaneis Rocha, and STF rulings on indirect elections in Rio de Janeiro and data sharing in the Marielle Franco case. Additional hearings include the Central Bank president and former Nubank executives.

item.title: STF decide nesta semana como serão as eleições no RJ
item.description: The STF will resume judgment on Wednesday (April 8, 2026) to decide whether the new governor of Rio de Janeiro will be chosen by indirect election by state deputies or by direct vote, following Claudio Castro’s resignation. So far, five ministers have voted to maintain indirect election with secret ballot, but may adjust their votes. The PSD, Eduardo Paes’ party, requested unification, citing a TSE precedent from 2018 in Tocantins that mandated direct election.

item.title: Programas sociais turbinados e isenção do IR: os bilhões que Lula vai injetar na economia em ano eleitoral
item.description: President Lula has implemented measures to expand social programs and exempt Income Tax for those earning up to R$ 5,000, while reducing the tax rate for those earning up to R$ 7,350, benefiting 15 million people. Initiatives like Gás do Povo and Luz do Povo are expected to inject between R$ 30 billion and R$ 40 billion into the economy, according to financial institution projections, aiming to boost purchasing power in an election year. Economists warn these measures could pressure inflation and hinder interest rate cuts by the Central Bank.

item.title: Para onde caminha o STF em relação às eleições no Rio de Janeiro
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) will decide on Wednesday whether the election for Rio de Janeiro's interim governor mandate will be direct in June (followed by another in October) or indirect, with a governor chosen by the State Legislative Assembly to serve until December 31. The ruling will determine the electoral model to fill the remainder of the state term. The article is exclusive to subscribers.

item.title: Direita racha, PL pressiona PSD e abre caminho para Gleisi no Paraná
item.description: The surprise affiliation of journalist Cristina Graeml with the PSD in Paraná has shaken the state's political landscape, following her departure from União Brasil, which had offered her a federal deputy seat — as had the PL. Her move opens the possibility for her to run for Senate or serve as vice on Governor Ratinho Jr.'s ticket, who leads the PSD in the state. This realignment weakens right-wing coordination in Paraná and could create opportunities for candidates like Gleisi Hoffmann of the PT by deepening divisions among opposition forces.

item.title: Prefeita petista desbanca ministro de Lula em disputa por Senado em MG
item.description: Former petista mayor of Contagem, Marília Campos, is leading Senate polls in Minas Gerais, challenging Minister Alexandre Silveira (PSD), who lost PT support and decided to run for reasons beyond loyalty to Lula. Her rise reflects the PSD's distancing from the federal government and the strengthening of the PT candidacy in the state.

item.title: Embaixador do Líbano pede papel mais ativo do Brasil para resolver conflito no Oriente Médio
item.description: Lebanon's ambassador to Brazil, Elias Nicolas, urged the Brazilian government to take a more active role in resolving the Middle East conflict, requesting a tangible financial contribution to the UN's $325 million humanitarian appeal and a meeting with President Lula and Foreign Minister Mauro Vieira. He emphasized historical ties between Brazil and Lebanon, noting 8 million Lebanese and descendants in Brazil and 22,000 Brazilians in Lebanon, with no reported Brazilian casualties, and warned of the migration crisis affecting over 1 million displaced people.

item.title: É #FAKE que Lula deu bolsa de R$ 7 mil por mês e moradia gratuita a refugiados muçulmanos no Brasil
item.description: The claim that the Lula government granted monthly stipends of R$ 7,000 and free housing to Muslim refugees is false. The video shared in the post actually shows the repatriation of Brazilians from Lebanon in October 2024, linked to Operation Acolhida, which assists Venezuelan migrants. The Ministry of Social Development confirmed there is no federal program offering benefits based on religion or nationality.

item.title: O cabo eleitoral de Messias que já declarou voto em Bolsonaro e apoia Caiado
item.description: AGU Minister Jorge Messias is relying on the support of evangelical bishop Samuel Ferreira, who declared his vote for Bolsonaro in 2022, to secure the 41 votes needed for his Supreme Court nomination. Ferreira is part of a group of evangelical leaders not aligned with Lula and Supreme Court ministers, including André Mendonça, Kassio Nunes Marques, and Cristiano Zanin, who are working behind the scenes in favor of Messias.

item.title: Promessa é dívida: com novo programa, Lula tenta evitar fim de mandato sem ‘desenrolar’ endividados
item.description: The Lula government launched a new program to reduce population indebtedness, aiming to avoid ending his term without fulfilling the campaign promise to 'unroll' the indebted. The initiative comes six months before the 2026 elections, seeking to improve the president's image and reduce rejection in polls. The article emphasizes that the program's success is crucial for Lula's re-election and to avoid breaking one of his main campaign promises.

item.title: Aprecie com moderação
item.description: Despite recent improvements in educational indicators such as Saresp and literacy rates in the second grade, results remain insufficient and below expectations, reflecting a pattern of modest progress in a system where assessments are calibrated too low. Electoral governments tend to exaggerate these gains with celebratory rhetoric, but experts warn this normalizes inadequate standards and overlooks that many students still fall short of basic proficiency, even by international benchmarks.

item.title: Tarcísio aposta em entregas do metrô para viralizar no ano eleitoral
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) is betting on the delivery of transportation infrastructure, particularly the Line 17-Gold of the São Paulo Metro, to strengthen his image in the 2026 electoral campaign, using humorous and viral videos on social media, including references to the 2014 World Cup mascot Fuleco and anime aesthetics. The strategy aims to counter the Workers' Party narrative, which emphasizes federal funding for the projects, while Tarcísio's allies believe his positive delivery agenda could secure a first-round victory against Fernando Haddad (PT), given the absence of other strong candidates.

item.title: PEC da Segurança segue parada no Senado um mês após passar na Câmara
item.description: The Public Security PEC (18/2025), approved in the Chamber of Deputies one month ago, remains stalled in the Senate due to the lack of a rapporteur designation by Senate President Davi Alcolumbre. The delay stems from growing tension between Alcolumbre and President Lula following the appointment of Jorge Messias to the STF, which undermined Alcolumbre’s preferred candidate. Unlike the Chamber, the Senate has no fixed procedure, giving Alcolumbre discretion to accelerate or delay the bill, which is a federal government priority in this election year.

item.title: As regras do jogo: o que muda para o Tribunal Superior Eleitoral em 2026 - O Assunto #1694
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) has released the definitive rules for the 2026 elections, banning payment for political electoral content on social media and reversing the burden of proof in cases of improper AI use, requiring producers of false content to technically prove no fraud occurred. The Court will be presided over by Minister Kassio Nunes Marques, with André Mendonça as vice-president, both appointed to the STF by Jair Bolsonaro and known for their discreet profiles. Additional rules restrict AI use three days before voting and prohibit platforms from suggesting candidates.

item.title: Filme traz de volta suspeitas sobre mortes na ditadura
item.description: The article revisits the Bolsonarist narrative linking João Goulart to communism due to his 1961 visit to China, coinciding with Jânio Quadros' resignation. It contrasts this with Richard Nixon, a U.S. leader involved in corruption scandals, who also pursued relations with China in 1972. The text suggests the 1964 military coup that toppled Goulart was backed and financed by the United States, offering a revisionist view of Brazil's dictatorship.

item.title: Quem são os governadores que deixaram mandatos para disputar a eleição
item.description: Eleven governors resigned from their offices to run in the 2026 elections, including two presidential candidates (Ronaldo Caiado and Romeu Zema) and eight seeking Senate seats. The resignation is required by law to prevent misuse of public resources in campaigns, with vice governors assuming the roles. Official candidacies will be confirmed in August after party conventions and TSE registration.

item.title: Sob pressão, governo promete editar MP de nova subvenção do diesel nos próximos dias
item.description: The Lula government promises to issue a provisional measure on diesel subsidies in the coming days, in partnership with states, to reduce the gap between international prices and domestic caps, with a fiscal impact estimated between R$ 3.5 and R$ 4 billion. The measure aims to overcome resistance from major distributors like Vibra (BR, Ipiranga, Raízen) and Petrobras, which initially rejected the first subsidy program, while only Rio de Janeiro and Rondônia have refused to join so far, with two to three other states still evaluating.

item.title: Estímulos à economia devem ultrapassar R$ 700 bi em 2026, ano eleitoral
item.description: In 2026, an election year, economic stimulus in Brazil is projected to reach R$ 742 billion, a 139% increase from 2025, equivalent to 5.4% of GDP.

item.title: CNDH pede investigação de juiz e advogado por homenagem ao golpe de 64
item.description: The CNDH has requested the OAB and TJ-PB to investigate a lawyer and a judge for honoring the 1964 military coup during an online session of the Criminal Chamber of the Paraíba State Court. The body considers the apology for the dictatorship a violation of human rights and the Brazilian Constitution.

item.title: Red Bull Bragantino vence de novo, e Mirassol completa 11 jogos sem vitória
item.description: Red Bull Bragantino defeated Mirassol 1-0 at Maião, ending Mirassol's 11-match winless streak in the 2026 Brasileirão. Mirassol sits at the bottom with six points and faces relegation threats, while Bragantino rises to 14 points in ninth place. The only goal came in the 50th minute after a corner kick and header by Isidro Pitta.

item.title: Dorival Júnior não resiste à nova derrota e é demitido do Corinthians
item.description: Corinthians fired head coach Dorival Júnior following a 1-0 loss to Internacional at Neo Química Arena, extending the team's winless streak to nine games. Dorival managed 63 matches with 25 wins, 19 draws, and 19 losses, leaving behind achievements including the 2025 Copa do Brasil and 2026 Supercopa Rei. Training on Monday will be led by William Batista, the under-20 team coach.

item.title: MPE recorre de decisão de Zanin que anulou condenação de Garotinho
item.description: The Electoral Public Prosecutor's Office appealed the interim decision by Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin that annulled the conviction of former governor Anthony Garotinho in the 'Chequinho' operation. Garotinho had been sentenced to 13 years and 9 months in prison for an alleged scheme to buy political support. The MPE's appeal seeks to overturn the suspension of the conviction's effects.

item.title: Lula 3 falha em popularidade porque perdeu a chance de surpreender
item.description: Lula’s third term maintains political stability and decent economic indicators, yet fails to generate electoral enthusiasm by not surprising Brazilians. His popularity is declining because he is perceived as maintaining the status quo rather than driving transformation, despite fulfilling basic promises and avoiding institutional crises.

item.title: Governo busca substituto para Gleisi; STF define regras para eleições no RJ
item.description: The government is seeking a replacement for Minister Gleisi Hoffmann following the departure of ministers who will run in the October elections, including Geraldo Alckmin. The STF has established the rules for the elections in Rio de Janeiro, setting guidelines for the state-level vote. The post-holiday week in Brasília will be marked by executive reorganization amid ongoing candidacies.

item.title: Trump estende prazo para o Irã reabrir estreito de Ormuz
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump extended the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz until 9:00 p.m. Brasília time on April 7, 2026, after initially setting a 48-hour deadline from April 4. The strait, through which about 20% of global oil flows, has been closed amid U.S.-Israeli military actions against Iran since February 28, 2026, causing global energy market volatility and rising fuel prices. Iran has refused to comply unilaterally, demanding political and security guarantees before any retreat, while Trump faces domestic pressure ahead of the November 2026 midterm elections.

item.title: Garotinho protagoniza propaganda partidária do Republicanos
item.description: Anthony Garotinho, former governor of Rio de Janeiro and 2026 pre-candidate for the Republicans party, starred in a party advertisement claiming it's time to put 'courageous people' to face a 'criminal gang' and return the state to the people. Despite being sentenced to 13 years for vote-buying in the Chequinho operation, his ineligibility was annulled by Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin, allowing him to run. A Gerp poll shows him with 8% voting intentions, tied for second place.

item.title: Renato Gaúcho vira assunto após derrota do Vasco para o Botafogo: ‘Incoerente’
item.description: Renato Gaúcho suffered his first loss as Vasco’s head coach after the team was overturned by Botafogo in the 10th round of the Brasileirão. The defeat sparked criticism on social media, with fans accusing him of incoherence and poor tactical preparation, particularly for not using all substitutions and lacking sufficient game analysis. Despite initial support, his tenure now faces scrutiny over whether he can sustain early success.

item.title: Janja cozinha paca para almoço de Páscoa com Lula; assista
item.description: First Lady Janja Lula da Silva prepared paca meat for President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's Easter lunch, sharing a video on Instagram. Lula praised the dish as 'divine' and said he had never tasted paca so delicious. Janja concluded the video by requesting to appear on Ana Maria Braga's morning show on TV Globo.

item.title: Janja, Lula e a carne de paca
item.description: First Lady Janja da Silva posted an Easter Sunday video showing her preparing paca meat for President Lula, stating it had been marinated for two days with green seasoning, garlic, and herbs. Lula appears in the video commenting on the dish, highlighting the familial and cultural context of the meal.

item.title: Brasiliense empata com o Primavera-MT na estreia da Série D 2026. Veja vídeo
item.description: Brasiliense drew 1-1 with Primavera-MT at home in their 2026 Série D opener. Regino scored for Brasiliense, while Giovanni equalized for Primavera-MT in the 26th minute. Brasiliense dominated possession and created multiple chances but failed to secure a win.

item.title: Após bate-boca entre Eduardo Bolsonaro e Nikolas Ferreira, Michelle compartilha vídeo de deputado
item.description: Michelle Bolsonaro shared a video by Nikolas Ferreira about the film 'The Passion of the Christ', following a social media clash between Eduardo Bolsonaro and Ferreira. Eduardo criticized Ferreira for disrespecting his family and allegedly working against former president Jair Bolsonaro, claiming the deputy has strayed from their shared values. The dispute is unrelated to the content Michelle reposted.

item.title: Michelle mostra almoço de Páscoa na casa dos Bolsonaros; assista
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Bolsonaro shared an Instagram video showing Easter lunch at the Bolsonaro residence in Brasília, highlighting a Le Creuset pot used to cook rice and cod. Former president Jair Bolsonaro has been under house arrest since March 27, following a 90-day grant by Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes. The Le Creuset pot featured in the video ranges from R$1,579 to R$4,399 on the brand’s website.
